headline: Washington posted a $10 million bounty on the Russian operatives who hijacked Signal and WhatsApp accounts as ShinyHunters turned Oracle's enterprise software flaws into a breach spree.

emerging_trends.text: ShinyHunters turned a string of Oracle enterprise flaws into an extortion engine within a single week, hitting Nissan, the National Association of Insurance Commissioners, and E-Business Suite users and showing how one vendor's bugs cascade across unrelated victims.
emerging_trends.topic_title: ShinyHunters Mine Oracle PeopleSoft and E-Business Suite

emerging_trends.text: Attackers keep poisoning the developer and browser supply chain, with Microsoft pulling 119 StegoAd Edge extensions and hijacked npm and Go packages slipping a Python infostealer onto Windows, macOS, and Linux hosts.
emerging_trends.topic_title: 119 Edge Extensions and Hijacked Packages Spread Malware

emerging_trends.text: Washington increasingly answers state hacking with cash, posting a $10 million reward for the Russian operatives who phished the Signal and WhatsApp accounts of United States officials and military leaders.
emerging_trends.topic_title: $10 Million Bounty for Russian Messaging-App Hackers

topics.title: $10 Million Bounty for Russian Messaging-App Hackers
topics.latest_developments: The U.S. State Department on June 29 posted a reward of up to $10 million for information identifying or locating members of UNC5792 and UNC4221, the groups tied to Russia's intelligence and military services behind the Signal and WhatsApp account takeovers.
topics.summary: UNC5792 and UNC4221 socially engineer their way into the messaging accounts of government officials, military leaders, and allied personnel, a campaign running since at least March 2026. Officials urge targeted users to lock down device-linking and backup recovery keys.

sources.title: US offers $10 million for info on group behind Signal and WhatsApp hacking spree
US posts $10 million reward over Russian cyber campaign targeting Signal, WhatsApp
U.S. offers $10 million for hackers targeting WhatsApp, Signal users
US Offers $10 Million Bounty for Russian State Hackers as Messaging App Attacks Evolve

topics.title: ShinyHunters Mine Oracle PeopleSoft and E-Business Suite
topics.latest_developments: Nissan disclosed June 29 that attackers exploiting an Oracle PeopleSoft zero-day stole current and former employee data, the National Association of Insurance Commissioners confirmed ShinyHunters breached its PeopleSoft server while the group claimed 3.1 terabytes, and Defused reported fresh exploitation of a separate critical Oracle E-Business Suite flaw, CVE-2026-46817.
topics.summary: The ShinyHunters extortion group chains Oracle enterprise software flaws to steal corporate and regulator data, with PeopleSoft and the E-Business Suite financial application now both under active attack. Affected organizations should apply Oracle's emergency fixes and hunt for data-theft indicators.

sources.title: Nissan discloses employee data breach linked to Oracle zero-day attacks
NAIC says public data stolen in ShinyHunters' PeopleSoft breach
Insurance Regulators Group NAIC Hit in Oracle PeopleSoft Hack
Hackers now exploit critical Oracle E-Business flaw in attacks

topics.title: SimpleHelp Flaw Deploys Djinn Stealer
topics.latest_developments: CISA added CVE-2026-48558, a critical authentication-bypass flaw in SimpleHelp remote-support software, to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities catalog June 29 as attackers used it to drop Djinn Stealer and the TaskWeaver loader.
topics.summary: Djinn Stealer is a previously undocumented cross-platform infostealer hitting Windows, macOS, and Linux, targeting cloud and AI credentials that link development and admin environments to wider enterprise systems. SimpleHelp operators must patch immediately under CISA's binding directive.

sources.title: 'Djinn' Stealer Targets Cloud, AI Credentials
Critical SimpleHelp flaw exploited to deploy new stealer malware
CISA Adds One Known Exploited Vulnerability to Catalog

topics.title: libssh2 and DirtyClone Open Clients and Kernels
topics.latest_developments: A public proof-of-concept dropped June 29 for CVE-2026-55200, a critical libssh2 flaw that lets a malicious SSH server corrupt a connecting client's memory without credentials or interaction, the same day SecurityWeek detailed DirtyClone, a DirtyFrag variant that gives unprivileged Linux users root by manipulating the page cache, and PTC's advisory confirmed JSP webshells dropping on unpatched Windchill instances.
topics.summary: CVE-2026-55200 affects every libssh2 release through 1.11.1 at CVSS 9.2, exposing any tool that links the client-side library, while DirtyClone and the Windchill CVE-2026-12569 webshells add local-root and remote-code-execution risk. Administrators should rebuild against patched libssh2, apply kernel fixes, and pull Windchill indicators of compromise.

sources.title: Public PoC Released for Critical libssh2 CVE-2026-55200 Client-Side SSH Flaw
'DirtyClone' Linux Kernel Vulnerability Leads to Root Access
JSP webshells being dropped on unpatched PTC Windchill instances

topics.title: 119 Edge Extensions and Hijacked Packages Spread Malware
topics.latest_developments: Microsoft removed 119 Edge Add-ons it ties to a single actor active since 2021, a campaign it calls StegoAd that hides payloads inside image and font files before waking days later to steal credentials and run ad fraud, while JFrog found two hijacked npm packages and a cluster of Go packages abusing VS Code tasks to deploy a Python infostealer.
topics.summary: Both operations smuggle malware past store and registry defenses, StegoAd through steganography in benign-looking files and the package attack by avoiding npm lifecycle scripts to dodge npm v12 hardening. Developers and users should audit installed extensions and pin dependency sources.

sources.title: Microsoft Removes 119 Edge Extensions That Hid Malware in Images and Fonts
Hijacked npm and Go Packages Use VS Code Tasks to Deploy Python Infostealer

topics.title: The Gentlemen Ransomware Crew Refines Its Backdoors
topics.latest_developments: Kaspersky researchers published June 29 an analysis of incidents tied to The Gentlemen ransomware-as-a-service group, disclosing its custom backdoors and tradecraft and flagging a new ransomware variant.
topics.summary: The Gentlemen runs a ransomware-as-a-service operation built around bespoke backdoors and evolving tactics. Kaspersky's tools, techniques, and indicators give defenders detection signatures for the group's intrusions.

sources.title: The Gentlemen are knocking: сustom backdoors and evolving tactics

games.recap: Ryan O'Hearn homers twice, helps Pirates avoid sweep with 9-4 win over Reds

games.result: Pirates 8 · Phillies 5 · Bot 8th (in progress at last update)

business_politics.title: Supreme Court Expands Trump's Removal Power, Spares the Fed
business_politics.latest_developments: The Supreme Court ruled June 29, letting President Trump fire the heads of independent agencies at will while blocking his attempt to remove Federal Reserve governor Lisa Cook.
business_politics.summary: The 6-3 decision exposes dozens of federal agencies to presidential control and strikes down long-standing for-cause removal protections; it carves out the Federal Reserve and keeps Cook in her seat after Trump moved to oust her.

sources.title: Supreme Court Blocks Trump's Bid to Fire Lisa Cook
The Supreme Court has handed Donald Trump yet more power

business.title: Rising Tide Partners' Distressed Properties Languish
business.latest_developments: KDKA reported June 29 that hundreds of distressed properties Rising Tide Partners bought to revive Pittsburgh neighborhoods still sit decaying five years on.
business.summary: Rising Tide Partners, a Pittsburgh nonprofit led by chief executive Diamonte Walker, has acquired hundreds of distressed properties since 2021 promising neighborhood revival; residents near its Homewood holdings say the buildings keep decaying.

sources.title: Rising Tide Partners has acquired hundreds of distressed properties. But residents say they're not seeing action.

business.title: Swissvale Businesses Brace for Parkway East Closure
business.latest_developments: WTAE reported June 29 that Swissvale business owner Dave Guerin warns a coming 25-day Parkway East closure will choke deliveries and supplier routes.
business.summary: A planned 25-day closure of the Parkway East, Interstate 376 east of Pittsburgh, threatens deliveries for businesses in Swissvale, where owner Dave Guerin says detours will complicate suppliers' access.

sources.title: "It's not gonna be good:' Local business braces for Parkway East closure

business.title: Allegheny County Moves to Block Deed Fraud
business.latest_developments: WPXI's 11 Investigates reported June 29 that Allegheny County filed court paperwork to halt deed transfers to the LLCs tied to a wave of fraudulent property transfers.
business.summary: Allegheny County is seeking a court order to block any deed transfers to limited-liability companies connected to recent deed-fraud cases, after a Pittsburgh real estate agent documented dozens of fraudulent transfers concentrated on the South Side.

sources.title: Allegheny County takes steps to prevent further deed fraud

around_town.title: Commercial Street Closes for Bridge Replacement
around_town.latest_developments: KDKA reported June 29 that Commercial Street officially closed as the bridge-replacement project near Frick Park entered its construction phase, a shutdown expected to last about five weeks.
around_town.summary: PennDOT closed Commercial Street in Pittsburgh's Swisshelm Park neighborhood for roughly five weeks so crews can replace the bridge carrying it near Frick Park, rerouting local traffic through surrounding streets.

sources.title: Commercial Street officially closes as bridge replacement project enters next phase

around_town.title: Justice Department Sues Pennsylvania Over SNAP Data
around_town.latest_developments: The U.S. Justice Department sued Pennsylvania, Kentucky, Michigan, and Minnesota on June 29 for refusing to hand the Agriculture Department data on food-stamp applicants.
around_town.summary: The Justice Department alleges the four states withheld five years of Supplemental Nutrition Assistance Program applicant records the U.S. Department of Agriculture sought to verify residents' eligibility and household benefit levels.

sources.title: DOJ accuses 4 states of refusing to provide SNAP data to federal officials

around_town.title: Monroeville Library Pulls Pride Display
around_town.latest_developments: WTAE reported June 29 that the Monroeville Public Library removed its Pride Month children's book display after local officials demanded it.
around_town.summary: The Monroeville Public Library took down a Pride Month book display in its children's room after Monroeville Councilman Bill Krut called the books 'sexual grooming' in a June 19 Facebook post, ending a rotation the library uses to mark different communities.

sources.title: Monroeville library removes Pride Month display after officials' request

around_teams.title: Steelers Face Joey Porter Jr. Contract Puzzle
around_teams.latest_developments: In a June 29 Post-Gazette video, beat writer Gerry Dulac weighed how complicated the Steelers' contract negotiations with cornerback Joey Porter Jr. could become.
around_teams.summary: Gerry Dulac broke down the looming extension talks between the Pittsburgh Steelers and cornerback Joey Porter Jr., assessing how complex a long-term deal for the young defender may prove.

sources.title: WATCH: How complicated will the Steelers' contract talks with Joey Porter Jr. be?

around_teams.title: Hiles: Pirates Should Look Within for Bullpen Help
around_teams.latest_developments: Post-Gazette columnist Noah Hiles argued June 29 that internal arms could fix the Pirates' bullpen better than trade-deadline acquisitions.
around_teams.summary: In his weekend column, Noah Hiles wrote that the Pittsburgh Pirates can shore up a shaky bullpen by leaning on relievers already in the organization, naming Dennis Santana and Isaac Mattson, ahead of the trade deadline.

sources.title: Noah Hiles' weekend thoughts: These internal options can help the Pirates' bullpen more than trades

team_usa.title: Sebastian Berhalter Scores in US Win Over Türkiye
team_usa.latest_developments: ESPN reported June 30 that Sebastian Berhalter scored his first World Cup goal in the United States' group-stage win over Türkiye, moving his father, former U.S. coach Gregg Berhalter, to tears.
team_usa.summary: Midfielder Sebastian Berhalter, son of former U.S. men's national team coach Gregg Berhalter, scored for the United States against Türkiye in the World Cup group stage, a goal that left his father, now Chicago Fire manager, in tears.

sources.title: Berhalter in tears after son Sebastian's WC goal

team_usa.title: Analysts See USMNT as World Cup Dark Horse
team_usa.latest_developments: ESPN convened coaches and analysts June 29 who judged the United States capable of a surprise deep run after a strong group stage as co-host.
team_usa.summary: A panel ESPN assembled of coaches and analysts weighed whether the United States men's national team can become the 2026 World Cup's surprise package, citing its group-stage form ahead of a round-of-32 meeting with Bosnia and Herzegovina on Wednesday, July 1.

sources.title: Why outsiders believe the USMNT can make a deep World Cup run

reading.author: Cal Newport
reading.title: Beware of Productivity Paradoxes
reading.summary: Newport argues that technologies poised to be productivity slam dunks, like the personal computer, often failed to deliver straightforward gains, a caution he applies to today's claims for AI.

reading.author: Stratechery
reading.title: An Interview with Figma CEO Dylan Field About Design and AI
reading.summary: Field discusses how he built Figma and why he believes AI works as a tailwind for the company rather than a threat to design tools.

reading.author: Ed Zitron
reading.title: Premium: Notes From The Bubble, Volume 1
reading.summary: Zitron launches an ongoing series cataloguing what he reads as mounting warning signs of an AI investment bubble across the tech industry.

sports_media: Not Just Football with Cam Heyward
